nodes.title: Understanding Your Business & Audience
nodes.rationale: Before any marketing efforts, it's crucial to have a clear understanding of your own business, its unique value proposition, and who your ideal customers are. This foundational knowledge will guide all subsequent marketing decisions.
suggested_micro_topics: Defining your business model and offerings
Identifying your Unique Selling Proposition (USP)
Creating customer personas
Market research basics
Competitor analysis for small businesses

nodes.title: Building a Basic Online Presence
nodes.rationale: For a small business in today's world, an online presence is non-negotiable. This module focuses on establishing the essential digital touchpoints where potential customers can find and interact with your business.
suggested_micro_topics: Choosing a domain name and web hosting
Setting up a simple website (e.g., using WordPress, Shopify, or a landing page builder)
Optimizing Google My Business profile
Creating essential website pages (About Us, Contact, Services/Products)
Ensuring mobile responsiveness

nodes.title: Introduction to Content Marketing
nodes.rationale: Content is the fuel for almost all digital marketing strategies. Learning how to create valuable content will attract, engage, and retain your target audience, establishing your business as an authority.
suggested_micro_topics: Understanding different content formats (blog posts, images, videos, infographics)
Developing a basic content calendar
Principles of effective copywriting for small businesses
Crafting compelling calls-to-action (CTAs)
Content distribution basics

nodes.title: Social Media Marketing Fundamentals
nodes.rationale: Social media provides a direct channel to connect with your audience, build brand awareness, and drive traffic. This module covers the basics of using social platforms effectively for your business.
suggested_micro_topics: Choosing the right social media platforms for your business (e.g., Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn)
Setting up and optimizing business profiles
Basic social media content creation and posting strategies
Engaging with your audience and community management
Understanding social media analytics basics

nodes.title: Search Engine Optimization (SEO) Basics
nodes.rationale: SEO helps your business appear higher in search engine results, making it easier for potential customers to find you organically when they search for products or services you offer.
suggested_micro_topics: Introduction to how search engines work
Basic keyword research for small businesses
On-page SEO fundamentals (titles, meta descriptions, headings)
Local SEO strategies (e.g., Google My Business optimization)
Understanding Google Search Console

nodes.title: Paid Advertising Introduction (Google & Social)
nodes.rationale: Paid advertising offers a faster way to gain visibility and drive targeted traffic to your business, complementing organic efforts. This module introduces the fundamentals of popular ad platforms.
suggested_micro_topics: Introduction to Google Ads (Search Campaigns)
Setting up your first Facebook/Instagram Ad campaign
Understanding ad targeting options for small businesses
Basic ad copy and creative best practices
Setting and managing a small advertising budget

nodes.title: Email Marketing Essentials
nodes.rationale: Email marketing remains one of the most effective direct communication channels for nurturing leads, building customer relationships, and driving sales for small businesses.
suggested_micro_topics: Strategies for building an email list (e.g., website forms, lead magnets)
Choosing an email service provider (e.g., Mailchimp, Constant Contact)
Designing basic email newsletters and promotional emails
Writing compelling subject lines
Understanding email automation basics (e.g., welcome sequences)

nodes.title: Marketing Analytics & Measurement
nodes.rationale: To ensure your marketing efforts are effective and to continuously improve, it's vital to track performance, understand what's working (and what isn't), and make data-driven decisions.
suggested_micro_topics: Introduction to Google Analytics 4 (GA4) for website tracking
Interpreting social media insights and reports
Analyzing paid ad campaign performance
Tracking email marketing metrics (open rates, click-through rates)
Defining key performance indicators (KPIs) for your business
